entries.title: President Trump and Democrats rarely find themselves in alignment. Yet both sides wanted the same outcome in Tuesday’s Texas Senate primary runoff election.
Candidates’ whiskers have emerged as a divisive issue in the race for California governor, reflecting a new bearded age in American politics.
State Attorney General Ken Paxton clinched the Texas Republican nomination for U.S. Senate, defeating longtime Sen. John Cornyn
The U.S. is scaling back the forces it plans to send to Europe in a crisis, a fresh step by the Trump administration to shrink its military support for NATO allies
The Trump administration announced a new green card policy Friday that, if carried out as intended, would force most applicants for permanent residency to leave the country and apply from abroad
Here are three political story lines to watch this week: Texas face-off, Trump’s health and the Iran talks. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
For more than a year, Republican senators had largely been deferential to President Trump‘s wishes. This week, they revolted over a Trump endorsement and his $1.776 billion “anti-weaponization fund.”
President Trump postponed signing an order on the dangers of AI, heeding an adviser’s warning that industry guardrails could slow down U.S. models in the race with tools from China
